query: map the equivalent of Azure Private Endpoint / AWS PrivateLink onto GCP — what's it called and does it behave the same

metrics.content:
**GCP equivalent:** **Private Service Connect (PSC)**.

It is conceptually closest to **Azure Private Endpoint** and **AWS PrivateLink / Interface VPC Endpoint**: a consumer VPC gets a **private IP endpoint** that reaches a Google API, partner/SaaS service, or producer-published service without using public internet paths.

**Not exactly the same behavior:**

| Concept | Azure | AWS | GCP |
|---|---|---|---|
| Consumer-side private endpoint | Private Endpoint NIC | Interface VPC Endpoint ENI | PSC endpoint / forwarding rule with private IP |
| Provider-side publishing | Private Link Service | Endpoint Service behind NLB | PSC Service Attachment behind internal LB |
| Approval workflow | Supported | Supported | Supported |
| DNS automation | Often integrated via Private DNS Zones | Optional private DNS for endpoints | More manual; Cloud DNS/private zones often required |
| Public access disabled automatically? | No | No | No |
| Regional behavior | Regional | Regional | Regional |

**Important nuance:** For Google APIs, you may also see **Private Google Access**, `private.googleapis.com`, or `restricted.googleapis.com`. Those are related private-access patterns, but **PSC is the closest architectural equivalent** to Azure Private Endpoint / AWS PrivateLink. For some services like Cloud SQL private IP, GCP may also use **Private Service Access**, which is VPC peering-based and not the same model as PSC.

Docs: [Private Service Connect](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/private-service-connect), [Private Google Access](https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/private-google-access).

> Analysis only — verify against vendor documentation before applying.
